fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Mississippi Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Mississippi - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity mississippi, how to transfer audiologist license to mississippi, audiologist compact mississippi, mississippi audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Mississippi Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.physical_address: 570 E. Woodrow Wilson Dr., Jackson, MS 39216

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Mississippi.

steps.title: Meet Educational Requirements
steps.description: Obtain a doctoral degree in audiology from an accredited educational institution.

steps.title: Pass the National Examination
steps.description: Pass the Audiology Examination (administered by Praxis).

steps.title: Apply for Licensure
steps.description: Submit a completed application and all required documentation to the Mississippi Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Mississippi audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Mississippi have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Mississippi participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Mississippi without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Mississippi?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Mississippi while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Mississippi
hero.dek: Mississippi Audiology license requires graduation from an accredited program, passing the national exam, and completion of supervised professional experience.
